dependencies: Electricity is the largest cost component in the production of atmospheric gases
Natural gas as primary raw material for hydrogen production via steam methane reformers
Helium supply dependent upon natural gas production by crude helium suppliers
Steel, aluminum, and capital equipment subcomponents such as compressors are principal raw materials in manufacturing of equipment
risks: Disruption in supply of energy, components, or raw materials due to market conditions, legislative or regulatory actions, natural disasters, public health crises, or other disruption
Lower natural gas production can reduce supplies of crude helium available for processing
Supply chain disruptions and related distribution delays and cost increases
Inability to find adequate sources of labor in locations where building new plants

regulatory_risks: European Union Emission Trading System coverage
California Cap-and-Trade Program
China's Emission Trading Scheme and nation-wide expansion
South Korea's Emission Trading Scheme
Netherlands CO2 emissions tax
Canada's Technology Innovation and Emission Reduction System (Alberta)
Ontario's GHG Emissions Performance Standards program
Singapore Carbon Pricing Tax Act
Taiwan Climate Change Response Act and carbon fee
U.S. EPA mandatory reporting of GHG emissions and regulation for new construction
European Union Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
European Union Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive
California Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act
California Climate Related Financial Risk Act
SEC Enhancement and Standardization of Climate-Related Disclosures for Investors
Compliance with CERCLA (federal Superfund law)
Compliance with Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA)
Environmental laws and regulations in countries of operation
Import/export restrictions
Anti-bribery and corruption laws (U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, UK Bribery Act, China Anti-Unfair Competition Law)
International sanctions and trade restrictions
Tax law changes in key jurisdictions
Global minimum taxes related to OECD framework
Increasingly aggressive enforcement of anti-bribery and anti-corruption requirements
Changes in tax laws and regulations and international tax treaties
FDA approval requirements
Competition laws and antitrust regulations

sanction_risks: U.S. and other jurisdictions' sanctions may prohibit or restrict business in certain countries
Tariffs and international sanctions in jurisdictions where we and affiliates operate
Government-imposed restrictions or expropriations in foreign jurisdictions
Russia's invasion of Ukraine causing project suspension and business divestiture
Potential sanctions impact on operations in Middle East
Export controls or regulatory restrictions preventing shipment to some markets
